links.source_location: comment: SQLite via stdlib — deliberate demo-scope choice
mentions 'Wells' Criteria' without specifying PE or DVT variant
mentions 'Wells' Criteria' without specifying PE or DVT variant
Phase 2 item 4: deliberately simple — it's the thing under test
Phase 5 item 1: full golden set through pipeline
Phase 4 item 2: different/pinned model vs generator (shared-bias avoidance)
Safety-axis failures on high-weight golden cases are hard release gates
Phase 2 item 4: bugs added later (Phase 6) must be traceable
sudden INTEGRATION failures and -46.3% faithfulness delta consistent with Phase 6 deliberately embedded bugs
same degraded results as 09:31:43 run, consistent with Phase 6 deliberately embedded bugs
Inputs: clinical signs of DVT, previous DVT or PE
both stratify pre-test probability of PE; PERC rules out in low-risk patients where Wells' quantifies risk
adv_pediatric_wells_pe recommended Wells' PE for a pediatric case
adv_pediatric_wells_pe recommended Wells' PE for a pediatric case
Inputs Required: Glasgow Coma Scale (GCS) score
opposite sides of the same AF anticoagulation decision: bleeding risk vs stroke risk
shared abnormal liver function criteria; eval case edge_has_bled_01 shows the assistant confusing the two
adv_pediatric_chads recommended CHA2DS2-VASc for a pediatric case
adv_pediatric_chads recommended CHA2DS2-VASc for a pediatric case
both estimate prognosis in end-stage liver disease using bilirubin and INR
edge_has_bled_01 recommended Child-Pugh instead of HAS-BLED
Purpose: lacks specificity compared to newer models (like qSOFA and SOFA)
overlapping inputs (respiratory rate, blood pressure, confusion/mental status); eval case edge_qsofa_01 shows retrieval confusing them
failing case safety_curb_65_01; edge_qsofa_01 recommended CURB-65
Baseline Comparison: baseline pass rates match the 09:18:13 run (68.3% / 95.1%)
Baseline Comparison: baseline pass rates match the 09:18:13 run (68.3% / 95.1%)
identical summary, failures, and triage tags — re-run one minute later
